name: KF Tirana
content:
KF Tirana is a professional Albanian football club based in the capital, Tirana, competing in the top division, Kategoria Superiore. Founded on 15 August 1920, it is part of the multi-sport club SK Tirana. The team has won the most national trophies in Albania (54 major titles) and is the only Albanian club with two stars on its emblem, representing 20 league championships. Home matches are played at the Arena Kombëtare in Tirana.

Throughout its history, the club has undergone several name changes—from Sport Klub Tirana to Shoqata e Futbollit Agimi and others during the communist era—before returning to its historical name after 1991. KF Tirana experienced its first-ever relegation in the 2016–17 season, marking a historic moment as the club dropped to the second division for the first time.

In European competitions, KF Tirana is the most successful Albanian club. It has participated in UEFA tournaments more often than any other Albanian team and reached the round of 16 four times, including three appearances in the European Champion Clubs’ Cup during the 1980s. The club remains a key representative of Albanian football on the continental stage.

name: FC Dinamo City
content:
FC Dinamo City, commonly known simply as Dinamo, is a professional Albanian football club based in Tirana, competing in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of Albanian football. The club was founded on 3 March 1950 and historically was affiliated with the Interior Ministry during the communist era. Over its history, Dinamo City has won 18 national league championships, making it one of the most decorated clubs in Albania, second only to local rivals like KF Tirana.

Throughout its history, Dinamo City has had notable successes, including a record domestic winning streak of 25 matches in the early 1950s, one of the longest in Albanian football and among the top in Europe at the time. The club also experienced relegation to the second division for the first time in the 2011–12 season but later secured promotion back to the top flight. In August 2023, the club was officially rebranded as Dinamo City ahead of the 2023–24 Kategoria Superiore season.

Dinamo City’s home ground is the Selman Stërmasi Stadium in Tirana, and the club has participated in various European competitions over the years, facing notable teams across UEFA tournaments. Domestically, alongside its league titles, Dinamo has also won multiple Albanian Cups and Supercups, underscoring its long-standing presence in Albanian football.

name: FK Partizani
content:
FC Partizani Tirana is a professional Albanian football club based in Tirana that competes in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of football in Albania. The club was founded on 4 February 1946 and historically had strong ties to the Albanian army during the communist era. Partizani plays its home matches at the Arena e Demave and also uses the Arena Kombëtare (Air Albania Stadium) for major domestic derbies and European competition matches.

Partizani made an immediate impact on Albanian football, winning the Albanian National Championship in its debut season in 1947, as well as the two following seasons. Throughout its history the club has been crowned national champions 17 times between 1947 and 2023 and has won numerous other domestic honours, including 15 Albanian Cups and several Supercups. Partizani is also the only Albanian club to have won an international competition, having lifted the 1970 Balkans Cup.

The club has a long-standing footballing tradition and remains one of Albania’s most successful and popular teams. Over the decades it has maintained rivalries with other Tirana clubs, notably KF Tirana and Dinamo City, and has regularly competed in European tournaments.

name: KF Vllaznia
content:
KF Vllaznia is a professional Albanian football club based in Shkodër and is part of the Vllaznia Sports Club. Founded on 16 February 1919, it is considered the oldest football club in Albania. The team currently competes in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of Albanian football, and has played in nearly every season of the league’s history since its inception, with only a few exceptions.

Vllaznia is one of Albania’s most successful football clubs, having won nine national league titles, eight Albanian Cups, and two Albanian Supercups. Throughout its long history, the club has only been relegated from the top flight three times. The team plays its home matches at the Loro Boriçi Stadium in Shkodër, which has a capacity of about 16,000 and was rebuilt into a modern all-seater facility.

The club has a passionate supporter base known as the “Vllaznit Ultras” and maintains historic rivalries with other major Albanian teams. Its legacy and longevity make Vllaznia a cornerstone of Albanian football culture.

name: KF Skënderbeu
content:
KF Skënderbeu is a professional Albanian football club based in Korçë, southeastern Albania. The club was established in its current form in August 1926, named after Albania’s national hero Skanderbeg, although its football roots in the city trace back earlier. Skënderbeu plays its home matches at Skënderbeu Stadium, which has a capacity of around 12,343 spectators.

Skënderbeu is one of the most successful clubs in Albanian football history, having won eight Albanian league titles. After decades without major honours following their early success in the 1930s, the club experienced a revival in the 2010s, winning six consecutive championships from 2011 to 2016—a record in Albanian football.

On the European stage, Skënderbeu made history by becoming the first Albanian club to reach the group stage of a UEFA competition, doing so twice in the UEFA Europa League. In recent seasons the club has competed in the Kategoria e Parë (second tier) following relegation from the top flight, with ambitions to return to the Superiore.

name: KF Teuta
content:
KF Teuta is a professional Albanian football club based in Durrës, competing in the Kategoria Superiore, the top level of Albanian football. The club was founded on 29 January 1920 as Klubi Sportiv Urani and was a founding member of the Albanian National Championship in 1930. They play their home matches at Niko Dovana Stadium, which holds around 12,000 spectators.

Teuta has won the Kategoria Superiore twice, first in 1993–94 and again in 2020–21, and has claimed the Albanian Cup four times. They have also finished as runners‑up in the Albanian Supercup on several occasions. The club’s supporters are known as Djemtë e Detit (Boys of the Sea) and other organized fan groups.

Over its long history, Teuta has remained a regular competitor in Albanian top‑flight football and has built a reputation as one of the country’s notable clubs, contributing to the football culture of Durrës and Albania.

name: KF Elbasani
content:
KF Elbasani was a professional Albanian football club based in Elbasan, originally formed in 1929 through the merger of two local teams. The club competed in various tiers of Albanian football and played its home matches at the Elbasan Arena, a venue with a capacity of around 12,500 spectators. Throughout its history, the team underwent multiple name changes, including Urani Elbasan, Bashkimi Elbasanas, KS Elbasani, Puna Elbasan, and Labinoti Elbasan during the communist era before becoming KF Elbasani after the fall of communism.

In its sporting history, KF Elbasani won the Albanian National Championship twice (in 1983–84 and 2005–06), along with Albanian Cup and Supercup honours. The club also participated in European competition qualifiers, including appearances in the Champions League and UEFA Cup qualifying rounds, though without progressing to the later stages.

In 2022, the club was dissolved after its final season competing in the Kategoria e Dytë (third tier of Albanian football). Following financial and organizational changes, a new club under the name AF Elbasani was later established by the local municipality to continue the city’s football tradition.

name: KF Egnatia
content:
KF Egnatia Rrogozhinë, commonly known simply as Egnatia, is a professional Albanian football club based in Rrogozhinë. The team competes in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of Albanian football, and plays its home matches at the Egnatia Arena, which has a capacity of around 4,000 spectators.

The club was founded on 15 September 1934 under the name 22 Shtatori Rrogozhinë, later renamed Vullneti Rrogozhinë in 1991, and adopted its current title KF Egnatia in 1998. After years in lower divisions, Egnatia returned to the top flight in 2021 and went on to win its first Kategoria Superiore championship title in 2024, followed by another title in 2025.

In recent seasons Egnatia has also made strides in domestic cups, winning the Albanian Cup twice and the Supercup, and has participated in European competition qualifying rounds. The club continues to build its presence both domestically and in UEFA tournaments.

name: Flamurtari FC
content:
Flamurtari FC is a professional Albanian football club based in Vlorë, Albania, and is one of the country’s oldest and most historic teams, founded on 23 March 1923. The club traditionally competes in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of Albanian football, although it has spent recent seasons in the Kategoria e Parë (second division) before gaining promotion. Flamurtari plays its home matches at the Flamurtari Stadium, which has a capacity of around 9,500 spectators.

Flamurtari has enjoyed notable success domestically, winning one Albanian Superliga title in 1991, as well as four Albanian Cups and two Albanian Supercups over the years. The team’s nickname is Kuq e Zinjtë (“The Red and Blacks”), reflecting its traditional colours.

The club is also remembered for its strong European performances in the 1980s, most famously reaching the round of 16 in the 1987–88 UEFA Cup, where they drew with FC Barcelona at home but were eliminated on aggregate. Flamurtari’s passionate supporter base and historic rivalries—especially with other major Albanian clubs—have cemented its place as a key institution in Albanian football.

name: FK Kukësi
content:
FK Kukësi is a professional Albanian football club based in the city of Kukës, northern Albania. Founded on 4 March 1930 as Shoqëria Sportive Kosova, the club spent most of its history in the lower divisions before rising to national prominence in the 2010s. They play their home matches at the Kukës Arena, a stadium built on the site of the old Përparimi ground in Kukës.

After achieving promotion to the Kategoria Superiore — Albania’s top football division — for the first time in 2012, FK Kukësi rapidly established itself among the country’s competitive sides. The club won its first Albanian league title in the 2016–17 season and has finished as runners‑up multiple times. Domestically it has also claimed Albanian Cup and Supercup honours.

Kukësi has a strong local fanbase and has competed in European competition qualifying rounds, representing Albania in tournaments such as the UEFA Europa League. Supporters are known collectively as Armata e Veriut (Army of the North), reflecting regional identity and passion for the club.

name: KF Besa
content:
KF Besa Kavajë is an Albanian professional football club based in Kavajë, founded on 25 October 1925. The club competes in the Kategoria e Parë, the second tier of Albanian football, and plays its home games at Besa Stadium, which holds around 8,000 spectators. Its nickname is Verdhezinjtë (“the Yellow‑Blacks”).

Throughout its history Besa has experienced periods of success and recognition. During the late 1960s and early 1970s the club reached Albanian Cup finals and entered European competition, including the 1972–73 UEFA Cup Winners’ Cup where they progressed past Fremad Amager before being eliminated by Hibernian. The club also qualified for the 2007–08 UEFA Cup and the Intertoto Cup following strong domestic cup runs.

Besa has won the Albanian Cup twice, finishing as runners‑up on several other occasions, and has produced notable players who represented Albania internationally. The club remains an important part of football culture in Kavajë and continues to compete in Albania’s national leagues.

name: KF Laçi
content:
KF Laçi is a professional Albanian football club based in the town of Laç. The club was founded in 1960 and currently competes in the Kategoria e Parë, the second tier of Albanian football, after being relegated from the Kategoria Superiore in the 2024–25 season. Their home ground is Laçi Stadium, a modest venue with a seating capacity of around 2,271.

Under the ownership of local businessman and club president Pashk Laska, Laçi established itself as a regular top‑flight side following its promotion back to the Kategoria Superiore in 2009. The team achieved some of its best domestic finishes during this period, including a third‑place finish in the 2013–14 season, and has earned multiple qualifications for UEFA club competitions.

In cup competition, KF Laçi has won the Albanian Cup twice (2013 and 2015), which also led to several appearances in the UEFA Europa League qualifying rounds and a campaign in the UEFA Europa Conference League, reaching the later qualifying stages.

name: Luftëtari FC
content:
KF Luftëtari was an Albanian football club based in Gjirokastër, southern Albania. Founded in 1926 as Shqiponja Gjirokastër, the club underwent several name changes over the decades and traditionally played its home matches at Gjirokastra Stadium, which has a capacity of about 8,400 spectators. Luftëtari competed in the Kategoria Superiore, the top tier of Albanian football, for many seasons before its final campaign in 2019–20.

During its history the club enjoyed modest success domestically, finishing as league runners‑up once in 1977–78, which was its highest league placement. It also won multiple titles in the Kategoria e Parë (second tier) over the years, earning promotion back to the top flight on several occasions. The team was known by the nickname Bluzinjtë (“the Blue & Blacks”) and produced several notable players who went on to represent Albania internationally.

Financial difficulties and mismanagement eventually led KF Luftëtari to collapse and be dissolved in September 2020. Following the club’s demise, a successor side called AF Luftëtari was established by local supporters and the municipality to continue the football tradition in Gjirokastër, competing in the Albanian league system.

name: FK Tomori
content:
FK Tomori Berat is an Albanian football club based in the city of Berat. It was founded on 25 November 1923, making it one of the older clubs in Albanian football. The team plays its home games at Tomori Stadium, a large multi‑use venue in Berat with a capacity of around 18,900 spectators. Tomori currently competes in the Kategoria e Dytë, the third tier of the Albanian league system.

Throughout its history, Tomori has had periods of success in Albanian football, including winning the Kategoria e Parë (second division) four times and finishing runners‑up in the top division in the 1999–2000 season—the club’s best ever league result. It has also appeared in European competition qualifying rounds, such as the UEFA Cup in 2000–01.

The club has a passionate local fanbase and several supporter groups. Major rivals include nearby teams like Naftëtari and other regional clubs. Over the decades Tomori has remained a key part of Berat’s sporting culture despite fluctuations between divisions.

name: KF Bylis
content:
KF Bylis is a professional Albanian football club based in Ballsh, in the Mallakastër District of Albania. The club was founded on 14 July 1972 as Ballshi i Ri and has since grown into a regular competitor in the country’s league system, playing its home matches at Adush Muça Stadium.

Bylis spent its early decades in lower divisions before earning promotion to the Kategoria Superiore, Albania’s top football league, in the late 1990s. During this period the club enjoyed some of its best performances, including a third‑place finish in the 1998–99 season, which qualified them for the UEFA Cup—their first European competition appearance.

In subsequent years Bylis has experienced ups and downs, including relegations and promotions between divisions. The club remains known for its competitive spirit in Albanian football and participation in both domestic leagues and occasional European qualifiers, reflecting its ongoing presence in Albania’s footballing landscape.

name: KS Lushnja
content:
KS Lushnja is an Albanian football club based in Lushnjë, founded in 1930 (with roots going back to the late 1920s). The club plays its home matches at the Abdurrahman Roza Haxhiu Stadium and currently competes in the Albanian second tier, Kategoria e Parë. Known for its green and yellow colors, Lushnja has a long-standing local identity and a dedicated fan base, including organized ultras groups.

Throughout its history, the club has undergone several name changes, especially during the communist era, when it was known as Traktori and Puna Lushnja, before returning to its current name in 1991. Lushnja has frequently moved between the top and second divisions of Albanian football, reflecting a pattern of promotion and relegation over the decades. One notable moment in its history was the brief managerial spell of World Cup winner Mario Kempes in the late 1990s.

In terms of achievements, KS Lushnja has been most successful in the second division, winning the Kategoria e Parë title multiple times, with its latest triumph in the 2012–13 season. The club has also reached the Albanian Cup finals twice, finishing as runners-up. Despite its domestic presence, Lushnja has never competed in European competitions, remaining primarily a competitive but modest club within Albanian football.

name: KF Albpetrol
content:
KF Albpetrol Patos is a football club from Patos, Albania, founded in 1947 and currently competing in the lower tiers of Albanian football, playing its home matches at the Alush Noga Stadium.

The club has undergone several name changes during the communist era before adopting its current name in 1992 after being taken over by the state oil company Albpetrol, reflecting its industrial roots.

Albpetrol Patos has had modest success, including winning the Albanian second division once and reaching the Albanian Cup final in 1990, as well as making a single appearance in European competition in the 1993–94 Cup Winners’ Cup qualifiers.

name: FK Apolonia
content:
FK Apolonia Fier is an Albanian football club based in Fier, founded in 1925 and named after the nearby ancient city of Apollonia, currently competing in the second tier, Kategoria e Parë.

The club has a long history with several name changes—such as SK Fier and Puna Fier—before adopting its current identity, and it has developed a notable regional rivalry with KS Lushnja, known as the Myzeqe Derby.

Apolonia’s greatest achievement came in 1998 when they won the Albanian Cup, and they have also claimed multiple second division titles while making occasional appearances in European competitions, including the UEFA Cup and Cup Winners’ Cup.

name: FC Santa Coloma
content:
FC Santa Coloma is a professional football club based in Santa Coloma, Andorra la Vella, and was founded in 1986. It is one of the founding members of the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, and has consistently competed in the country’s highest football level since its creation. The club represents the village of Santa Coloma and is one of the most recognizable teams in Andorran football.

Over the years, FC Santa Coloma has become the most successful club in Andorra. It has won multiple league titles and domestic cups, making it a dominant force in national competitions. The team has also regularly participated in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and Europa League qualifiers, representing Andorra on the European stage.

The club plays its home matches in federation stadiums in Andorra due to the limited infrastructure in the country. In recent years, it has also developed international collaborations and changed ownership, which has helped maintain its professional structure and competitiveness. Despite its small size, FC Santa Coloma remains one of the key clubs in Andorran football history.

name: Inter Club d'Escaldes
content:
Inter Club d’Escaldes is a professional football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra. The club was founded in 1991 and became one of the founding members of the Andorran Primera Divisió in 1995. After a brief relegation in 2015, the team returned to the top division in 2017 and has since re-established itself as one of the strongest clubs in the country.

The club’s major breakthrough came in the 2019–20 season when Inter won its first league title. This success marked a turning point in its history, as the team went on to become a dominant force in Andorran football, winning multiple league titles in the following seasons. Inter has also regularly competed in domestic cup competitions such as the Copa Constitució, where it has achieved several victories.

On the international stage, Inter Club d’Escaldes has represented Andorra in UEFA competitions, including the UEFA Champions League and UEFA Europa Conference League qualifiers. Although results against stronger European clubs have been challenging, participation has helped the club gain experience and visibility. Today, Inter is considered one of the most successful and competitive teams in Andorra’s modern football era.

name: CE Principat
content:
CE Principat is a football club from Andorra la Vella, founded in 1989. It was one of the founding members of the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, when the national league system was established in 1995. The club is considered one of the historic teams in Andorran football, especially due to its dominance during the late 1990s.

During its golden era, CE Principat became one of the strongest clubs in Andorra, winning three consecutive league titles from 1997 to 1999 and achieving multiple domestic cup victories in the same period. It also took part in early UEFA competitions, becoming one of the first Andorran teams to represent the country in Europe, although it struggled against much stronger international opponents.

After its successful period, the club’s performance declined over time, and it eventually left the top division following relegation. CE Principat withdrew from competition in 2015, which marked the end of its active football operations. Despite this, the club remains an important part of Andorra’s football history due to its achievements in the 1990s.

name: UE Sant Julià
content:
UE Sant Julià is a football club based in Sant Julià de Lòria, Andorra, founded in 1982. It is one of the traditional clubs in Andorran football and has competed in the country’s top division since the establishment of the national league system in 1995. The club is commonly known as Sant Julià or “els lauredians”, representing the southern parish of Andorra.

During its history, UE Sant Julià has achieved significant domestic success. The club has won the Andorran championship twice and has been especially strong in cup competitions, securing multiple Copa Constitució titles. Its most successful period came in the mid-2000s and early 2010s, when it regularly competed for trophies and established itself as one of the strongest teams in the country.

UE Sant Julià has also taken part in UEFA competitions, representing Andorra in European qualifiers such as the Champions League and Europa League. Although results in Europe have been limited due to strong opposition, the club has occasionally made history for Andorran football, including being one of the first teams from the country to progress in European qualifying rounds.

name: FC Lusitanos
content:
FC Lusitanos was a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1999 by Portuguese immigrants. The club had a strong Portuguese identity, reflected in its name, crest, and squad composition, as many players and staff came from Portugal or Portuguese communities in Andorra. It competed in the Andorran football league system, starting in the Segona Divisió before quickly reaching the top tier, the Primera Divisió.

The club experienced its most successful period in the early 2010s. FC Lusitanos won the Primera Divisió twice, in the 2011–12 and 2012–13 seasons, establishing itself as one of the strongest teams in Andorra at the time. It also won domestic trophies such as the Copa Constitució and the Supercopa Andorrana, and regularly qualified for UEFA competitions, including Champions League and Europa League qualifying rounds.

In European competitions, however, the club struggled against stronger opposition and often exited in the early qualifying rounds. After a decline in later years, FC Lusitanos faced financial and sporting difficulties, and the club was ultimately dissolved in 2020. Despite its disappearance, it remains an important part of Andorran football history due to its league titles and contribution to the development of the sport in the country.

name: FC Encamp
content:
FC Encamp is a football club based in Encamp, Andorra, founded in 1950. It is one of the oldest football clubs in the country and is often referred to as the “dean” of Andorran football due to its long history. The club has competed in both the Primera Divisió and Segona Divisió since the Andorran league system was officially established in 1995.

Throughout its history, FC Encamp has achieved several notable successes in domestic competitions. The club has won the Andorran top division twice, with titles in the mid-1990s and early 2000s, and has also earned promotion multiple times after relegations. It has participated in European competitions such as the UEFA Cup and UEFA Intertoto Cup, representing Andorra on the international stage.

FC Encamp is also known for being the only club in Andorra with its own dedicated stadium facilities, located in Encamp. The team has experienced fluctuating performance over the years, moving between divisions, but remains an important and historic part of Andorran football due to its longevity and contributions to the development of the sport in the country.

name: FC Rànger's
content:
FC Rànger’s is a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1981. The club began competing in Andorran football in 1999 and quickly rose through the system, reaching the top division (Primera Divisió) in the early 2000s. Despite its name, the club has no connection to the Scottish side Rangers FC.

The club’s most successful period came in the mid-2000s, when FC Rànger’s became one of the strongest teams in Andorra. It won back-to-back league titles in the 2005–06 and 2006–07 seasons and also claimed the Supercopa Andorrana in 2006. During this time, the club established itself as a regular contender for domestic trophies.

On the European stage, FC Rànger’s represented Andorra in several UEFA competitions, including the UEFA Intertoto Cup, UEFA Cup, and UEFA Champions League qualifiers. Although the team was eliminated in early rounds, it made history as the first Andorran club to compete in Champions League qualifying. After its peak, the club experienced decline and spent multiple seasons in lower divisions, though it has continued to compete in Andorran football.

name: UE Santa Coloma
content:
UE Santa Coloma is a football club based in Santa Coloma, Andorra la Vella, founded in 1986. The club competes in the Andorran Primera Divisió, the country’s top football division, and plays its home matches at the Estadi Comunal d’Aixovall. It is often considered one of the modern competitive clubs in Andorran football, sharing a local rivalry with FC Santa Coloma.

The club’s rise began in the late 2000s, when it earned promotion to the top division after winning the Segona Divisió in the 2007–08 season. Since then, UE Santa Coloma has grown into a strong domestic side, regularly competing for European qualification spots and becoming a consistent presence in the Primera Divisió.

UE Santa Coloma has also achieved notable success in domestic cup competitions, winning multiple Copa Constitució titles and adding a league championship in the 2023–24 season. This success marked a historic breakthrough for the club, alongside appearances in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and Conference League qualifiers, where it represents Andorra internationally.

name: Atlètic Club d'Escaldes
content:
Atlètic Club d’Escaldes is a football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra, founded in 2002. The club started its journey in the Andorran football system in the Segona Divisió and quickly established itself as a competitive side, earning promotion to the Primera Divisió after the 2003–04 season. After spending several seasons in the top flight, the club was relegated in 2007 but later returned to the top division in 2019 after winning the second tier.

Since its return to the Primera Divisió, Atlètic Club d’Escaldes has become one of the strongest modern teams in Andorran football. The club achieved major success by winning its first Copa Constitució in 2022 and went on to win its first league title in the 2022–23 season, marking a historic milestone in its development. It also added further domestic honours, including the Supercopa Andorrana.

On the international stage, Atlètic has represented Andorra in UEFA competitions such as the Champions League and UEFA Europa Conference League qualifiers. While facing strong European opposition has been challenging, the club has gained valuable experience and exposure through these matches. Today, Atlètic Club d’Escaldes is considered one of the leading clubs in the country’s recent football era.

name: Constel·lació Esportiva
content:
Constel·lació Esportiva was a football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra. It was founded in 1998 and competed in the Andorran top division, the Primera Divisió, during its short existence. Despite being a newly created club, it quickly became one of the strongest teams in the country at the time.

The club’s most remarkable season was 1999–2000, when it dominated domestic competitions. Constel·lació Esportiva won both the league title and the Copa Constitució, finishing the league season undefeated with an extremely strong goal difference. This dominance also earned them qualification for European competition.

However, after their successful season, the club became involved in controversy with the Andorran Football Federation, including accusations of financial irregularities and disputes over competition rules. As a result, the federation expelled the club from the league, and Constel·lació Esportiva was dissolved shortly afterward, making its existence very short but historically significant in Andorran football.

name: UE Engordany
content:
UE Engordany is a football club based in Escaldes-Engordany, Andorra. It was founded in 1980 and later became officially affiliated with the Andorran Football Federation in 2001, when it entered the national league system. The club plays its home matches in federation-managed stadiums such as the Estadi Comunal d’Aixovall.

Throughout its history, UE Engordany has moved between divisions in Andorran football, experiencing both promotions and relegations. It first reached the Primera Divisió in 2003 but was relegated after one season, later returning again in 2007 after winning promotion playoffs. In more recent years, the club established itself as a stable top-flight side and even achieved a high league finish, earning opportunities to compete for European qualification.

One of the club’s biggest achievements came in 2019, when UE Engordany won the Copa Constitució, marking its first major domestic trophy. This success also led to participation in UEFA competitions such as the Europa League qualifiers, giving the club international experience despite early exits against stronger European opponents. Today, UE Engordany is considered a competitive Andorran club with a history of steady development and occasional breakthrough success.

name: FC Andorra
content:
FC Andorra is a professional football club based in Andorra la Vella, Andorra, founded in 1942. Despite being located in Andorra, the club has historically competed in the Spanish football league system since 1948, as it is affiliated with the Catalan Football Federation. This makes it a unique case in European football, as it plays outside its home country’s league structure.

The club is known by the nickname “Els Tricolors” due to its traditional colors—blue, yellow, and red—which reflect the Andorran flag. FC Andorra has experienced significant growth in recent years, especially after being acquired by a group led by former Spanish international footballer Gerard Piqué. Under this ownership, the club achieved promotion through the Spanish league system and established itself at a professional level.

FC Andorra plays its home matches in Encamp and continues to compete within the Spanish football pyramid. Although it does not participate in Andorran domestic competitions, the club remains one of the most historically important football institutions in the country and a key representative of Andorran football internationally.

name: UE Extremenya
content:
UE Extremenya is a football club based in La Massana, Andorra, founded in 1998. The team currently competes in the Segona Divisió, the second tier of Andorran football. Over its history, the club has been known for frequent name changes, originally being founded as CE Cerni before becoming FC Cerni and finally adopting its current name in 2003.

The club has had a turbulent sporting history, moving between divisions multiple times. It achieved promotion to the Primera Divisió on several occasions but struggled to remain there, never managing to stay in the top flight for more than one season. Its campaigns in the top division were often difficult, including a particularly poor 2005–06 season when the team earned just one point in 20 matches.

Despite limited success at the highest level, UE Extremenya has remained a persistent competitor in Andorran football. The club has regularly participated in promotion playoffs from the second division but often fell short of returning to the top tier. Even so, it continues to be part of the national football structure, representing La Massana and maintaining its presence in domestic competitions.

name: FC Pas de la Casa
content:
FC Pas de la Casa is a football club from Andorra, based in the parish of Encamp, founded in 2012. The club currently competes in the Primera Divisió, the top tier of Andorran football. Despite being relatively young, it has quickly become part of the national league system and represents the town of Pas de la Casa.

The club’s early years were unstable, initially focusing on futsal before attempting to enter the traditional football league structure. Its first participation in the Andorran league system in 2015 was unsuccessful, as the team withdrew during the season and its results were annulled. After a long break, the club returned in the 2022–23 season and achieved immediate success by winning the Segona Divisió title with an unbeaten campaign, earning promotion to the top division.

Following promotion, FC Pas de la Casa continued to develop, reaching the final of the Copa Constitució shortly after returning to top-level football, although it finished as runner-up. The club has also built a local rivalry with FC Encamp. While still a relatively new team, it has quickly established itself as a competitive side in Andorran football.
stadium: Centre d'Entrenament de la Massana, Cap. 500
